name: WhatsApp Vision Memory (describe media once, remember forever)

parameters.content:
## ⬅ Drop YOUR vision / transcription model here

Replace this note with the AI node you already use — e.g. an **OpenAI** "Analyze Image", a **Google Gemini** vision node, or a **Whisper** transcription node. Feed it the binary `data` field from **Download File** and have it output a short description/transcript.

SocialMate ships **no** model — it just stores what your model produces.

## Vision Memory

**Why:** a customer sends a photo, an invoice, a voice note. Your AI analyzes it — but on the next turn, `get_ai_context` only sees a bare `[image]` and pays to analyze it again. This loop **describes each media item exactly once** and caches the result in SocialMate (Agent Memory, **Pro**).

**Flow:** `media.discovered` fires → **Needs analysis?** skips items already described (`needsContext:false`) → **Download File** pulls the bytes → **your own model** describes them → **Set Media Context** saves the description.

From then on, that media comes back to any agent as `[image: <description>]` inside **Get AI Context** — no re-analysis, fewer tokens.

**Setup:**
1. One **SocialMate API** credential (admin-scope key so the Trigger can self-register). Server URL `http://127.0.0.1:3456` or your named-tunnel host.
2. Swap the yellow note for the AI node you use; map its text output into **Set Media Context → Context**.
3. **Save & Activate**, then send the number a photo to test.
